title: Chicken 65 (Crispy, Juicy, Authentic)

Chicken & Marinade: Boneless chicken thigh – 600 g, cut into 1½-inch cubes (Thighs preferred for juiciness; breast can be used if brined)
Kashmiri red chilli powder – 2 tsp (color without excessive heat)
Fresh green chilli paste – 1 tsp (adjust to heat preference)
Fresh curry leaf paste (or finely crushed) – 1 tbsp

Method.title: 1. Marination (Critical Step)
steps: In a stainless-steel bowl, combine all marinade ingredients except oil.
Add chicken and massage thoroughly for 2–3 minutes until tacky.
Add 1 tsp oil and mix again—this locks in moisture.
Cover and rest:
Minimum: 45 minutes
Ideal: 6–8 hours under refrigeration
Professional tip: Longer marination allows enzymes and acids to tenderize without breaking texture.

Method.title: 2. Frying (Golden & Crisp)
steps: Heat oil to 175–180°C.
Fry chicken in small batches to avoid temperature drop.
Fry for 4–5 minutes, turning once, until:
Deep golden red
Crisp exterior
Internal temp reaches 74°C
Remove and rest on a wire rack (not paper towel).

Method.title: 3. Aromatic Tempering (Restaurant Signature)
steps: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wide sauté pan.
Add garlic; sauté until lightly golden (do not brown).
Add green chillies and curry leaves—stand back.
Add chilli powder and a pinch of sugar.
Toss fried chicken quickly on high heat (30–40 seconds).
Finish with lemon juice. Switch off immediately.

Chef Notes: Rice flour + egg white = long-lasting crispness
Thigh meat prevents dryness during high-heat frying
Tempering must be fast and hot to avoid sogginess
Plating & Presentation: Serve hot in a shallow bowl
Optional accompaniment: onion rings or mint yogurt dip
